summary: Research on the brown alga *Sargassum horneri* demonstrated its therapeutic potential in mitigating atopic dermatitis (AD)-like skin lesions in murine models, primarily through the suppression of the Th2 cytokine interleukin-13 (IL-13). Extracts of *S. horneri* significantly reduced IL-13-mediated immune cell infiltration, including T cells, dendritic cells, and macrophages, while also decreasing collagen deposition in the skin. These findings indicate that *S. horneri* possesses bioactive compounds capable of modulating inflammatory pathways associated with AD. The study was conducted in a controlled laboratory setting, with no specific geographic or ecological scope beyond the use of this edible brown algal species.
